_meta.name: writing_v1
_meta.description: Golden writing-quality cases per kind (thank-you letter, newsletter, recipient update, quarterly summary). Each case provides the grounding facts the draft must stick to and the workspace voice rules it must respect. Graders: faithfulness (figures grounded), voice (no banned terms), readability (plain English), structure (title + body + sections), plus an LLM rubric judge (warmth / specificity / clarity-CTA / on-voice).

cases.goal: Draft a warm thank-you letter to Margaret Henderson for her gift.
cases.criteria: Names Margaret Henderson, cites the $10,000 gift and the 48 young readers, sounds genuinely grateful, ends with a clear invitation to stay involved, and never uses the banned term 'child'/'children'.

context.title: Thank you, Margaret
context.prompt: Thank Margaret Henderson for her generous gift and share the impact.
retrieved_context: Margaret Henderson donated $10,000 on 2026-05-15 to the Zaylan Foundation.
The Zaylan Foundation runs a literacy program supporting 48 young readers in East Africa.
The program provided 320 books to young readers in the last term.

cases.goal: Draft the September 2026 supporter newsletter for the Zaylan Foundation.
cases.criteria: Covers the period September 2026, cites $50,000 raised across 12 gifts and 3 newly enrolled young readers, is structured with clear sections, reads at plain-English level, and ends with a clear call to action.

context.title: September 2026 Impact Update
context.prompt: Summarise September 2026 fundraising and program impact for supporters.
retrieved_context: In September 2026 the Zaylan Foundation raised $50,000 from 12 gifts.
3 new young readers were enrolled in the literacy program in September 2026.
The program now supports 48 young readers in total.
An upcoming reading day is scheduled for October 2026.

cases.goal: Draft an update about the young reader Amina for her sponsor.
cases.criteria: Is about Amina, cites that her reading level improved by 2 grades this term, is specific and warm, and never uses the banned term 'child'.

context.title: Amina's progress this term
context.prompt: Share Amina's reading progress with her sponsor.
retrieved_context: Amina is a young reader enrolled in the Zaylan Foundation literacy program.
Amina's reading level improved by 2 grades this term.

cases.goal: Draft a Q3 2026 program summary for the board.
cases.criteria: Covers Q3 2026, cites $120,000 raised and 48 young readers supported, is clear and well-structured, and stays on-voice.

context.title: Q3 2026 Program Summary
context.prompt: Summarise Q3 2026 fundraising and program reach for the board.
retrieved_context: In Q3 2026 the Zaylan Foundation raised $120,000.
The literacy program supported 48 young readers in Q3 2026.
